2605.22364.title: Scaling Observation-aware Planning in Uncertain Domains
2605.22364.paper_summary: Deciding which sensing capabilities to deploy on an agent in uncertain domains is a fundamental engineering challenge, in which one balances task achievability against the high costs of hardware and processing. This problem has previously been formalized as the Optimal Observability Problem (OOP), based on the well-known Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP) model for decision-making. This work studies (sub-)symbolic techniques to scale solving of decidable fragments of the OOP, namely the Sensor Selection Problem (SSP) and the Positional Observability Problem (POP). Besides improving the original approach based on parameter synthesis, we develop a new solving method that identifies sensible observation functions via decomposition of POMDPs, improving performance by 3 and 5 orders of magnitude for instance size and runtime, respectively.
2605.22364.paper_summary_zh: 在不確定領域中決定在代理上部署哪些感測能力是一項基本的工程挑戰，這需要在任務可達性與硬體和處理的高成本之間取得平衡。這個問題之前已經被形式化為最佳可觀察性問題（OOP），基於著名的部分可觀察馬可夫決策過程（POMDP）模型進行決策。本研究探討（子）符號技術，以擴展可解片段的解決方案，即感測器選擇問題（SSP）和位置可觀察性問題（POP）。除了改進基於參數合成的原始方法外，我們還開發了一種新的解決方法，通過對POMDP的分解來識別合理的觀察函數，從而在實例大小和運行時間上分別提高了3和5個數量級的性能。
2605.22364.author: Adrian Zvizdenco et.al.
2605.22364.authors: Adrian Zvizdenco, Arthur Conrado Veiga Bosquetti, Alberto Lluch Lafuente, Christoph Matheja
